steps.text: Ensure conferences are managed in a transparent, objective, and unbiased manner.
Adhere to CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences when applicable.
Provide documentation of a plan with a timeline for the meeting's substantive involvement and content development.
Provide a written process for reviewing presentations or abstracts and selecting them based on criteria.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.
Establish a written process for approving conference funding.
Review and approve proposals by the CC/CO/NC.
Document the review and approval of conference funding.

constraints.text: Review must be conducted by a committee composed of 3 staff members or peers per meeting.
Final approval is made by the CC/CO/NC Director.

steps.text: Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval.
After reviewing the full final conference agenda and other conference information, provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor after final approval is provided.

constraints.text: No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

steps.text: Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG prior to approving any request or issuing any obligation documents.
Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility stating that facilities, goods and services are fully ADA compliant.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
Maintain documentation of the review of ADA obligations.
